name: Malaysia

visa_types.name: Single Entry Visa (Social Visit)
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign nationals who require a visa to enter Malaysia for a social/tourist visit. One entry only.
visa_types.notes: Valid 3 months from issue date. Applicants must apply via MyVISA (malaysiavisa.imi.gov.my) or through a Malaysian embassy abroad. Not available to nationals of Israel or North Korea via standard channels. Applicable to all nationalities that require a visa for Malaysia.

visa_types.name: Multiple Entry Visa (Social Visit) - Indian Nationals
visa_types.purpose: Allows Indian nationals to make multiple visits to Malaysia for social/tourist purposes within the visa validity period.
visa_types.notes: Fee: MYR 100. Valid 1 year from issue date; maximum 30 days per entry. For Indian citizens only. Tour groups are not eligible. Requires proof of sufficient funds and valid confirmed return ticket. Note: Indian nationals currently enjoy visa-free access until 31 December 2026, so this MEV is an alternative for those needing multi-entry documentation.

visa_types.name: Multiple Entry Visa (Social Visit) - Chinese (PRC) Nationals
visa_types.purpose: Allows Chinese (PRC) nationals to make multiple visits to Malaysia for social/tourist purposes within the visa validity period.
visa_types.notes: Fee: MYR 30. Valid 1 year from issue date; maximum 30 days per entry. For PRC citizens only. Tour groups not eligible. Requires proof of sufficient funds and valid confirmed return ticket. Note: Chinese nationals enjoy mutual visa exemption (30 days per entry, max 90 days per 180-day period) in force since 17 July 2025.

visa_types.name: Multiple Entry Visa (Business/Government Affairs)
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign nationals requiring multiple visits to Malaysia for business, trade, or official government matters.
visa_types.notes: Valid 3–12 months from issue date (up to 1 year). Issued via MyVISA portal or Malaysian embassy abroad. Maximum stay per entry determined by immigration officer on arrival. Tour groups not eligible.

visa_types.name: Transit Visa
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign nationals passing through Malaysia en route to another destination who need to clear immigration.
visa_types.notes: Not required if traveller remains airside (does not clear immigration) and continues on the same flight. Transit visa needed only if entering Malaysia between connecting flights. Apply via MyVISA portal or Malaysian embassy abroad.

visa_types.name: Visa With Reference (VDR) - Social Visit
visa_types.purpose: A visa requiring pre-approval from Immigration Department headquarters before issuance by a Malaysian Representative Office abroad; typically for nationals of restricted countries or spouses of Malaysian citizens seeking entry.
visa_types.notes: Applies to: spouses of Malaysian citizens, and citizens from Afghanistan, India (when visa exemption not in force), China, Vietnam, North Korea, Cuba, and other restricted countries. Application submitted through Malaysian embassy/high commission abroad; forwarded to IMI HQ for approval before visa is issued. Spouses of Malaysians must show sponsor income of at least MYR 2,000/month.

visa_types.name: Employment Pass (EP) - Category I
visa_types.purpose: Issued to highly-skilled foreign professionals employed by Malaysian companies in senior/specialist positions with high salary thresholds.
visa_types.notes: Managed by Expatriate Services Division (ESD). Issued via Xpats Gateway (MDEC-registered companies) or ESD portal. Requires prior approval from Expatriate Committee (EC) or relevant regulatory agency. Fee: MYR 800 + 6% SST = MYR 848. Three EP categories based on salary level; salary thresholds revised effective June 2026. EP holders may bring Dependant Pass holders. Valid up to 5 years.

visa_types.name: Employment Pass (EP) - Category II
visa_types.purpose: Issued to skilled foreign professionals employed by Malaysian companies at mid-level salary thresholds.
visa_types.notes: Same application process as Category I via ESD. Salary thresholds revised June 2026. Valid up to 2 years. Succession plan mandate to facilitate knowledge transfer to local staff introduced from June 2026.

visa_types.name: Employment Pass (EP) - Category III
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign workers employed by Malaysian companies at entry-level professional salary thresholds.
visa_types.notes: Valid up to 1 year (renewable). Same ESD application process. Salary thresholds revised June 2026. Holder may bring dependants on Dependant Pass.

visa_types.name: Visitor's Pass (Temporary Employment)
visa_types.purpose: Temporary work authorization for foreign nationals engaged in short-term employment or contract work in Malaysia.
visa_types.notes: Also covers foreign domestic helpers (maids). Short-term in nature; specific validity and fees depend on the category and employer type. Managed by Immigration Department.

visa_types.name: Professional Visit Pass (PVP)
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign professionals conducting temporary professional or technical activities in Malaysia, including training, installation, or consultancy work.
visa_types.notes: This is also the pass type used for the DE Rantau Digital Nomad programme. Maximum 12 months per grant; renewable. Applicants are sponsored by a Malaysian company or organisation. Applied through ESD or IMI.

visa_types.name: DE Rantau Nomad Pass
visa_types.purpose: Allows qualified foreign digital nomads (tech and non-tech professionals) to live and work remotely in Malaysia for non-Malaysian employers.
visa_types.notes: Issued as a Professional Visit Pass (Pas Lawatan Ikhtisas). Duration: 3–12 months initial stay, renewable once for up to additional 12 months (max 24 months total). Fee: MYR 1,000 for main applicant, MYR 500 per dependent. Tech roles minimum USD 24,000/year income; non-tech roles minimum USD 60,000/year income. Open to all nationalities except Israel. Applicants can bring spouse, children (under 18), and parents. Programme operated by Malaysia Digital Economy Corporation (MDEC). Apply at malaysiadigital.mdec.my.

visa_types.name: Student Pass
visa_types.purpose: Issued to non-citizen students (aged 3 and above) enrolled in approved Malaysian educational institutions from pre-school to higher education.
visa_types.notes: Fee: MYR 60. Higher education applicants must apply via EMGS (Education Malaysia Global Services) STARS system or EMGS portal, and must be outside Malaysia when applying. Valid for duration of studies. Post-arrival medical exam required within 7 days for higher education students. University students may work up to 20 hours/week at approved venues (restaurants, petrol stations, hotels, universities) only. Dependants at Master's/PhD level may accompany on Social Visit Pass (MYR 90) but cannot work. Valid passport must have at least 18 months validity and health insurance of at least 12 months coverage is required.

visa_types.name: Dependant Pass
visa_types.purpose: Issued to immediate family members (spouse and children) of Employment Pass or Professional Visit Pass holders to allow them to reside in Malaysia during the primary pass holder's tenure.
visa_types.notes: Validity tied to primary Employment Pass holder's pass. Dependant Pass holders generally cannot work without converting to an appropriate work pass. Children and spouses of EP holders are the primary eligible group. Applied through ESD together with or after the main EP application.

visa_types.name: Long Term Social Visit Pass (LTSVP)
visa_types.purpose: Issued to family members of Malaysian citizens or permanent residents for extended residence in Malaysia, including spouses, step-children, elderly parents, and dependants of foreign students.
visa_types.notes: Minimum 6-month stay authorisation. Spouses of Malaysian citizens may receive up to 5 years validity and are permitted to work, conduct business, or engage in professional employment without converting to an Employment Pass. Categories include: spouses of citizens, children/stepchildren of citizens under 7 years, elderly without home-country family but with local family support, immediate family of foreign students, medical patients, spouses of permanent residents, and widows/widowers of deceased citizens. Application submitted at state Visa, Pass and Permit Division with original documents. Applications submitted at a Malaysian embassy abroad are not typically accepted for this pass type.

visa_types.name: Malaysia My Second Home (MM2H) - Silver Category
visa_types.purpose: Long-term social visit pass allowing eligible foreign nationals to reside in Malaysia for an extended period under the entry-tier MM2H programme.
visa_types.notes: One of four MM2H tiers managed by Ministry of Tourism, Arts and Culture (MOTAC). Programme does not lead to permanent residence or citizenship. Financial requirements (fixed deposit amounts, liquid asset thresholds) apply per the January 2026 Terms and Regulations but specific amounts not publicly accessible from official web pages (mm2h.gov.my returns HTTP 403 on category pages). Non-official sources indicate approximately MYR 675,000 fixed deposit for Silver but this is unverified from official source. Pass is typically valid 5 years (renewable). Spouses and children may be included as dependants.

visa_types.name: Malaysia My Second Home (MM2H) - Gold Category
visa_types.purpose: Long-term social visit pass for the mid-tier MM2H programme, allowing foreign nationals to reside in Malaysia with higher financial requirements than Silver.
visa_types.notes: Second of four MM2H tiers under MOTAC. Programme does not lead to permanent residence or citizenship. Financial requirements not text-extractable from official pages (403 errors). Pass typically valid 5 years renewable. Dependants (spouse and children) may be included.

visa_types.name: Malaysia My Second Home (MM2H) - Platinum Category
visa_types.purpose: Long-term social visit pass for the highest-tier MM2H programme, targeting high-net-worth individuals seeking extended residence in Malaysia.
visa_types.notes: Highest MM2H tier under MOTAC. Programme does not lead to permanent residence or citizenship. Financial requirements (highest among tiers) not text-extractable from official pages (403 errors). Pass typically valid 5 years renewable. Holders may be eligible for additional benefits vs lower tiers.

visa_types.name: Malaysia My Second Home (MM2H) - SEZ/SFZ Category
visa_types.purpose: Long-term social visit pass for investors in Malaysia's Special Economic Zones or Special Financial Zones under the MM2H framework.
visa_types.notes: SEZ/SFZ-specific MM2H category for investors in designated economic zones. Specific financial requirements not accessible from official web pages. Programme under MOTAC and does not lead to PR or citizenship.

visa_types.name: Malaysia Premium Visa Programme (PVIP)
visa_types.purpose: A premium 20-year renewable long-term visa for high-net-worth individuals who wish to live, work, and study in Malaysia, requiring a significant fixed deposit and offshore income.
visa_types.notes: Requires: MYR 1,000,000 fixed deposit in a Malaysian licensed bank (up to 50% may be withdrawn after 1 year for approved purposes); minimum offshore income of MYR 40,000/month; government participation fee MYR 200,000 for main applicant, MYR 100,000 per dependant. Pass valid 20 years, renewed every 5 years. Holders may work and study in Malaysia. Processing time: 30 working days. Managed by Immigration Department (IMI). Does not lead to permanent residence or citizenship. Excludes Israeli nationals.

visa_types.name: Residence Pass - Talent (RP-T)
visa_types.purpose: A long-term residence pass for highly-skilled foreign professionals and talent who have been working in Malaysia, providing greater flexibility and longer-term stay rights.
visa_types.notes: Valid 10 years. Issued to highly-skilled foreign professionals meeting talent criteria set by Immigration Department. Holder may work for any employer in Malaysia without changing pass. Full eligibility criteria and financial requirements not detailed on accessible official pages during research. Applied through IMI.

visa_types.name: Long Term Social Visit Pass (Medical Treatment)
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign nationals who need to remain in Malaysia for extended medical treatment at approved healthcare facilities.
visa_types.notes: Sub-category of the Long Term Social Visit Pass specifically for medical patients. Duration tied to treatment period. Application typically requires medical documentation from a Malaysian licensed hospital or clinic. Applied at state Visa, Pass and Permit Division.

visa_types.name: Border Pass (Malaysia-Indonesia / Malaysia-Thailand)
visa_types.purpose: Allows residents of border areas between Malaysia and Indonesia or Malaysia and Thailand to cross the border for short-term visits without a standard visa.
visa_types.notes: Bilateral arrangement for border communities. Specific eligibility (residence within a defined km of border), validity, and fees are governed by bilateral agreements. Only valid for use at designated border crossing points and for travel within a specified border zone. Not available to general public.

visa_types.name: APEC Business Travel Card (ABTC)
visa_types.purpose: Allows pre-cleared business travellers from APEC economies to enter Malaysia (and other participating ABTC economies) for short business visits without requiring individual visas.
visa_types.notes: ABTC holders can enter Malaysia via dedicated ABTC lanes at international entry points. Card valid 5 years. Must be a citizen of an ABTC-participating economy and meet business traveller criteria. Applications submitted through the home economy's ABTC administrator. Malaysia is a full ABTC participant.

visa_free.notes: Unrestricted visa-free access; no duration cap per the official IMI page. Singapore citizens may stay beyond one month without a visa.
Unrestricted visa-free access; no duration cap per the official IMI page. Brunei citizens may stay beyond one month without a visa.
ASEAN member. Visa not required for stays of less than one month.
ASEAN member. Visa not required for stays of less than one month.
ASEAN member. Visa not required for stays of less than one month.
ASEAN member. Visa not required for stays of less than one month.
ASEAN member. Visa not required for stays of less than one month.
ASEAN member. Visa not required for stays of less than one month.

Visa exemption for short-term social visits effective 1 October 2025. Reciprocal arrangement. 14-day maximum stay. Entry subject to approval by immigration officer at designated international entry/exit points. Myanmar was previously excluded from the ASEAN visa exemption bloc and required a visa.

Visa exemption valid until 31 December 2026 per official imi.gov.my. 30 days maximum per entry. Exemption is for social/tourist visits only. Extended from December 2023 exemption. Citizens of India may also apply for Multiple Entry Visa (MEV) valid 1 year with 30 days per entry.

Permanent mutual visa exemption agreement in force 17 July 2025 (signed 16 April 2025). Covers holders of ordinary passports (both Malaysian and PRC) and PRC public affairs passports. 30 days per visit, maximum 90 days cumulative within any 180-day period. This is a permanent bilateral treaty, not time-limited. Applies to Mainland China only; excludes Hong Kong, Macau, and Taiwan.
Visa-free for social/tourist visits. 90-day maximum stay per entry per current IMI policy for most visa-exempt nationalities.

No visa required for tourism or business for stays of 90 days or less. US citizens receive a Social Visit Pass on arrival. Confirmed by US State Department: 'No visa is required for 90 days or less if you visit for tourism or business.' Longer stays or other purposes require advance visa approval.

UAE citizens do not require a visa for social visits not exceeding 90 days. Confirmed by the Embassy of Malaysia in Abu Dhabi and Consulate General of Malaysia in Dubai (kln.gov.my official pages): 'Citizens of United Arab Emirates (UAE) do not require a visa to enter Malaysia for any social visit not exceeding 90 days.'
Visa-free for social/tourist visits. 90-day maximum stay.
Visa-free for social/tourist visits. 90-day maximum stay. Re-confirmed 2026-06-29 via UK government official travel advice (gov.uk): 'You can normally be allowed to stay for 90 days on arrival.'

e_visa.nationality: All nationalities requiring a visa for Malaysia (excluding applicants from Malaysia, Singapore, Israel, and North Korea)
e_visa.notes: Effective 15 November 2021, the MyVISA (eVISA) system is available to all nationalities that require a visa to enter Malaysia. Applications must be submitted from outside Malaysia, Singapore, Israel, and North Korea. The eVISA applies for social visit, employment-related, and other pass types. Official portal: malaysiavisa.imi.gov.my

visa_policy.notes: Malaysia grants visa-free access to a large number of nationalities (the exact count exceeds 51 - see gaps). Most visa-exempt nationalities receive up to 90 days per visit. ASEAN nationals (except Myanmar) receive 30 days under the ASEAN framework; Brunei and Singapore have no stated duration limit. Myanmar has a separate bilateral 14-day visa exemption effective 1 October 2025. India has a temporary 30-day exemption (social/tourist) until 31 December 2026. China (PRC) has a permanent mutual bilateral visa exemption agreement in force 17 July 2025 (30 days/entry, max 90 days/180-day period). The United States and UAE are confirmed visa-free at 90 days (added 2026-06-29). eNTRI for India and China were pilot programs from 2017 that expired; they are superseded by the current exemptions. Visa-on-arrival is not an official Malaysia entry category per imi.gov.my. The official IMI visa-requirement-by-country page requires a live fetch to enumerate all countries; the list in this record is known to be incomplete (see data_quality.gaps). Re-verified against imi.gov.my (timed out), kln.gov.my (SSL errors), travel.state.gov, and gov.uk on 2026-06-29.

conditional_access.conditions: Bilateral Agreement between Malaysia and Bangladesh on the Partial Abolition of Visa Requirement for Holders of Diplomatic and Official Passports (2014). Visa-free for social visit purpose only; visit pass issued on arrival for a stay not exceeding 30 days. Visa still required for study, work, or training purposes. Ordinary Bangladesh passports require a visa.

Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) on the Mutual Short-Stay Visa Exemption for Diplomatic and Official or Special Passport Holders, signed 10 December 2024 in Riyadh. Maximum stay duration not stated in the official press release. Ordinary Saudi Arabian passports require a visa.

cbi.notes: Malaysia does not offer a Citizenship by Investment (CBI) programme. No official government source provides any pathway to citizenship through investment. Source: absence of any program on imi.gov.my, motac.gov.my, or mm2h.gov.my.

programs.name: Malaysia My Second Home (MM2H) - Silver Category
programs.notes: One of three MM2H tiers (Platinum, Gold, Silver) plus a SEZ/SFZ category. Programme run by Ministry of Tourism, Arts and Culture (MOTAC). Financial requirements not directly extractable from official government pages (mm2h.gov.my returns HTTP 403 on category pages). Non-official aggregators indicate FD requirements around MYR 675,000 (Silver) but this cannot be confirmed from an official page. Programme does not lead to permanent residence or citizenship.

programs.name: Malaysia My Second Home (MM2H) - Gold Category
programs.notes: Second-tier MM2H category. Financial requirements not directly extractable from official pages (403 errors). Programme managed by MOTAC. Does not lead to permanent residence or citizenship.

programs.name: Malaysia My Second Home (MM2H) - Platinum Category
programs.notes: Highest MM2H tier. Financial requirements not directly extractable from official pages (403 errors). Programme under MOTAC. Does not lead to permanent residence or citizenship.

programs.name: Malaysia My Second Home (MM2H) - SEZ/SFZ Category
programs.notes: Category for investors in Special Economic Zones / Special Financial Zones. Specific requirements not extractable from official pages (403 errors). Programme under MOTAC.

programs.name: Malaysia Premium Visa Programme (PVIP)
programs.notes: Requires MYR 1,000,000 fixed deposit in a Malaysian licensed bank (max 50% withdrawal after 1 year for approved purposes). Government participation fee: MYR 200,000 for main applicant; MYR 100,000 per dependent. Minimum offshore income of MYR 40,000/month. Visa valid for 20 years, renewable every 5 years. Pass holders may work and study in Malaysia. Processing time: 30 working days. Managed by Immigration Department (imi.gov.my). Does not lead to permanent residence or citizenship.

programs.name: DE Rantau Nomad Pass
programs.eligibility: Foreign nationals (all nationalities except Israel) aged 18+. Tech Talent/Profession: minimum USD 24,000 annual income from non-Malaysian sources. Non-Tech Talent/Profession: minimum USD 60,000 annual income from non-Malaysian sources. Must have active contract of 3+ months duration with foreign/non-Malaysian employer.
programs.processing_time: Not specified on official MDEC page
programs.notes: Issued as a Professional Visit Pass (Pas Lawatan Ikhtisas). Duration: 3–12 months initial stay, renewable for up to additional 12 months (max 24 months total). Fee: MYR 1,000 for main applicant, MYR 500 per dependent. Programme operated by Malaysia Digital Economy Corporation (MDEC).

programs.name: Employment Pass (EP)
programs.eligibility: Foreign nationals employed by Malaysian companies for skilled/professional positions. Applications through Xpats Gateway (MDEC-registered companies) or ESD.
programs.notes: Categories I, II, and III based on salary level. Managed through Expatriate Services Division (ESD). Fee: MYR 800 + 6% SST = MYR 848 for Employment Pass. Salary thresholds revised effective June 2026. Succession plan mandate introduced June 2026.

programs.name: Residence Pass - Talent (RP-T)
programs.eligibility: Highly-skilled foreign professionals meeting talent criteria set by Immigration Department.
programs.notes: Long-term residence pass for talent. Listed on imi.gov.my main services/pass page. Specific eligibility and financial criteria not fully detailed on pages accessible during research.

agreements: ASEAN (Association of Southeast Asian Nations) - visa-free movement for ASEAN nationals for stays under 1 month (Brunei and Singapore: unrestricted; Cambodia, Indonesia, Lao PDR, Philippines, Thailand, Vietnam: 30 days); Myanmar now has a separate bilateral 14-day exemption effective 1 October 2025
Malaysia-Bangladesh Bilateral Agreement on Partial Abolition of Visa Requirement for Holders of Diplomatic and Official Passports (2014)
Malaysia-Saudi Arabia MoU on Mutual Short-Stay Visa Exemption for Diplomatic and Official or Special Passport Holders (signed 10 December 2024)

Malaysia-China Mutual Visa Exemption Agreement for holders of ordinary passports (Malaysia) and ordinary + public affairs passports (PRC) - in force 17 July 2025; 30 days per visit, max 90 days per 180-day period
Malaysia-Myanmar mutual visa exemption for short-term social visits (14 days) - effective 1 October 2025

validation_notes: ADDED visa_types array: Research conducted June 2026 from official imi.gov.my, esd.imi.gov.my, mdec.my, and imigresen-online.imi.gov.my. Covers all major visa and pass categories including tourist (Single/Multiple Entry, VDR), transit, work (EP Cat I/II/III, PVP, Visitor Temp), digital_nomad (DE Rantau), student, family (Dependant Pass, LTSVP), retirement (MM2H tiers), investment (PVIP), medical (LTSVP-Medical), and special programs (Border Pass, ABTC).

ADDED Myanmar (MMR) as visa_free with 14-day max stay: Malaysia-Myanmar mutual visa exemption effective 1 October 2025 per kln.gov.my/mmr_yangon official announcement. Prior record incorrectly excluded Myanmar entirely from the visa-free list.

CORRECTED max_stay_days for most visa-free countries from null to 90 days: Corroborated by multiple sources indicating Malaysia grants up to 90 days to most exempt nationalities for social/tourist visits. Prior record left these as null/unspecified.

REMOVED eNTRI entries from eta[]: Both eNTRI programs (India and China) were pilot programs valid only in 2017 and are long expired. The official imi.gov.my eNTRI pages still exist but describe a 2017 program that ended in 2018 (India) and December 2017 (China). Listing these as active eta entries was incorrect. India and China entry is now covered by the broader visa-free exemptions.

CORRECTED ASEAN group entry: Split the former combined 'ASEAN nationals' group entry into individual country entries for clarity and accuracy, since Myanmar (previously excluded) now has its own bilateral exemption and Brunei/Singapore have different conditions.

RETAINED China CHN entry in visa_free: The mutual visa exemption (in force 17 July 2025) supersedes the expired eNTRI for Chinese nationals. Max stay confirmed as 30 days per entry, up to 90 days in any 180-day period. Agreement is permanent (bilateral treaty signed 16 April 2025), not time-limited to 2026.

RETAINED India IND entry in visa_free: The exemption extended until 31 December 2026 per IMI official page. Max stay is 30 days. This is a temporary exemption (not a bilateral treaty), expiry 31 December 2026.

UPDATED conditional_access eligible_nationalities field: Changed from 'any' to explicit iso3 array ['BGD'] and ['SAU'] respectively, since these bilateral agreements apply only to those specific nationalities.
CONFIRMED e_visa entry unchanged: MyVISA (eVISA) effective November 2021 per esd.imi.gov.my announcement remains accurate.
CONFIRMED cbi has_program=false: No citizenship by investment program exists in Malaysia.
MM2H program URLs corrected from category-specific paths to main mm2h.gov.my since all category pages return HTTP 403.

Noted that the motac.gov.my published a January 2026 Terms and Regulations PDF for MM2H which exists but could not be text-extracted; category-specific financial amounts remain unverifiable from official sources.
Employment Pass salary thresholds revised effective June 2026 per esd.imi.gov.my; succession plan mandate also introduced June 2026.

RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-27: Re-fetched imi.gov.my/visa-requirement-by-country. IMI page confirms 51 named visa-free countries plus ASEAN (handled separately) plus Myanmar (14-day bilateral). Visa-required list confirmed includes Afghanistan, Angola, Bangladesh, Bhutan, Burkina Faso, Burundi, Cameroon, CAR, Colombia, DRC, Congo, Djibouti, Equatorial Guinea, Eritrea, Ethiopia, Ghana, Guinea-Bissau, India (with exemption note until Dec 2026), Ivory Coast, Liberia, Mali, Montenegro, Mozambique, Nepal, Nigeria, Niger, Pakistan, Rwanda, Serbia, Sri Lanka. India exemption re-confirmed at 30 days until 31 Dec 2026 via IMI page. China bilateral agreement re-confirmed as permanent treaty via IMI FAQ and Chinese Embassy sources. Myanmar 14-day bilateral re-confirmed via web search corroborating kln.gov.my/mmr_yangon announcement. UPDATED China source_url from kln.gov.my/chn_beijing (SSL error) to imi.gov.my FAQ page. UPDATED China notes to clarify the agreement is a permanent bilateral treaty. No visa_free entries added or removed in this run.

RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-29: imi.gov.my main page timed out during this session; kln.gov.my pages return SSL certificate errors on all WebFetch attempts. ADDED United States (USA) to visa_free list at 90 days: confirmed by US State Department travel advisory (travel.state.gov - official US government) which states 'No visa is required for 90 days or less if you visit for tourism or business.' ADDED United Arab Emirates (ARE) to visa_free list at 90 days: confirmed via official Malaysian Embassy Abu Dhabi and Consulate General Dubai pages (kln.gov.my domains) - search snippets from those pages state 'Citizens of United Arab Emirates (UAE) do not require a visa to enter Malaysia for any social visit not exceeding 90 days.' UPDATED United Kingdom (GBR) source_url to gov.uk/foreign-travel-advice/malaysia which explicitly confirms 90-day visa-free entry. India and China exemptions re-confirmed via web search citing imi.gov.my snippets. NOTE: Major gap identified - the visa_free list is likely still significantly incomplete. Multiple widely-known visa-free nationalities (all EU member states, GCC beyond UAE, South Korea, Turkey, Brazil, Russia, Argentina, Mexico, etc.) are not recorded in the file because the IMI official page was inaccessible and kln.gov.my had SSL errors this session. See gaps.

SOURCE NOTE 2026-06-27: kln.gov.my pages for Beijing and New Delhi returned SSL certificate errors during this run; data for those entries was verified via IMI official page (imi.gov.my) and web search corroborating kln.gov.my content. kln.gov.my remains official but SSL issues prevent direct fetch.
gaps: MM2H category-specific financial requirements (Platinum, Gold, Silver, SEZ/SFZ minimum fixed deposit amounts) not text-extractable from any accessible official government page - mm2h.gov.my returns HTTP 403 on category pages; MOTAC PDF (January 2026) is binary-only and not text-extractable by available tools

kln.gov.my pages return SSL certificate errors on direct WebFetch (confirmed again on 2026-06-27 re-verification run). Myanmar 14-day exemption confirmed via web search returning kln.gov.my/mmr_yangon snippet as official source plus corroborating travel industry reports. The kln.gov.my domain remains the authoritative Malaysian MFA source; SSL error is a fetch tooling limitation, not a data quality issue.

Complete definitive list of all visa-exempt countries with stay durations is not rendered in a parseable format on imi.gov.my (page timed out on 2026-06-29 re-verification). The visa_free list is significantly incomplete. Major countries widely reported as 90-day visa-free but NOT YET in the file include: EU member states (France DEU/FRA, Germany DEU, Italy ITA, Spain ESP, Austria AUT, Belgium BEL, Denmark DNK, Finland FIN, Sweden SWE, Norway NOR, Portugal PRT, Greece GRC, Poland POL, Czech Republic CZE, Hungary HUN, Romania ROU, Bulgaria BGR, Croatia HRV, Slovakia SVK, Slovenia SVN, Estonia EST, Latvia LVA, Lithuania LTU, Luxembourg LUX, Malta MLT already in file, Cyprus CYP already in file, Iceland ISL, Liechtenstein LIE already in file); GCC states beyond UAE (Kuwait KWT, Bahrain BHR, Oman OMN, Qatar QAT - Saudi Arabia only has diplomatic access in conditional_access); South Korea (KOR); Turkey (TUR); Argentina (ARG); Brazil (BRA); Mexico (MEX); Russia (RUS); and many others. Wikipedia (citing Timatic) lists these as visa-free but official Malaysian government sources could not be directly accessed this session to confirm per country. These must be verified against a live imi.gov.my page before adding.

Complete definitive list of all 50 visa-exempt countries with stay durations is not rendered in a parseable format on imi.gov.my; the 90-day figure for most countries is supported by general IMI policy pattern but exact per-country durations should be verified from the rendered HTML table
Saudi Arabia MoU (Dec 2024): maximum stay duration for diplomatic/official/special passport holders not stated in the official kln.gov.my press release

eNTRI current status for India and China: official imi.gov.my pages for eNTRI remain online but describe expired 2017 programs. No official announcement of program termination was found; pages may be archived/outdated without being formally retired
DE Rantau processing time not available on official MDEC page
RP-T (Residence Pass - Talent) full eligibility criteria not detailed on accessible official pages
Broader diplomatic/official/service passport exemption list beyond Bangladesh and Saudi Arabia is not comprehensively documented on any accessible official Malaysian government page
PVIP program amounts confirmed from official IMI FAQ PDF but PDF is binary-only; text confirmation relies on prior research session
India exemption: whether the 30-day exemption will be renewed beyond 31 December 2026 is unknown; no official announcement as of June 2026
Employment Pass revised salary thresholds (June 2026) not specifically enumerated in text-extractable form from esd.imi.gov.my (403 errors on direct category pages)
Transit visa: specific fee, maximum airside duration, and list of nationalities requiring it not enumerated on accessible official pages
Visa With Reference: fees and validity not stated on the IMI page; processing time not specified
Border Pass bilateral arrangements: specific eligibility zones, fees, and permitted entry points not text-extractable from official sources

sources_checked.description: Malaysia Immigration Department - Visa Requirement by Country (fetched June 2026)
eNTRI for Indian Nationals - IMI (confirms program was April 2017 - March 2018 only; expired)
eNTRI for Chinese PRC nationals - IMI (confirms program was Jan-Dec 2017 only; expired)
IMI announcement: MyVISA extended to all nationalities requiring visa (Nov 2021)
IMI announcement Dec 2023: SEV exemption for Chinese and Indian tourist nationals
IMI FAQ: Malaysia-China Mutual Visa Exemption
Embassy of Malaysia, Beijing - Mutual Visa Exemption Agreement (ordinary + public affairs passports) in force 17 July 2025
Embassy of Malaysia, Yangon - Visa exemption announcement for Myanmar passport holders effective 1 October 2025 (SSL error on direct fetch; confirmed via search snippet)
High Commission of Malaysia, Dhaka - FAQs confirming Bangladesh diplomatic/official passport agreement (30 days, social visit only)
MFA press release - Malaysia-Saudi Arabia MoU on visa exemption for diplomatic/official/special passports (10 December 2024)
Official MM2H programme website - Ministry of Tourism, Arts and Culture (HTTP 403 on category pages)
MOTAC - Malaysia My Second Home Programme announcement page (confirmed 4 tiers: Platinum, Gold, Silver, SEZ/SFZ)
MOTAC - MM2H Terms and Regulations January 2026 PDF (exists but binary-only, not text-extractable)
Official IMI FAQ PDF for Malaysia Premium Visa Programme (PVIP)
MDEC - DE Rantau Nomad Pass official page (fetched June 2026)
IMI - Pass types overview (fetched June 2026)
IMI - Types of visas: Single Entry, Multiple Entry (India/China MEV), Transit Visa details and fees (fetched June 2026)
IMI - Student Pass requirements, fees (MYR 60), eligibility, EMGS process (fetched June 2026)
IMI - Long Term Social Visit Pass categories and eligibility including spouse of citizen, medical, elderly (fetched June 2026)
IMI - Visa With Reference (VDR): definition, eligible categories, restricted nationality requirement (fetched June 2026)
ESD - Expatriate passes including Employment Pass (Cat I/II/III), Dependant Pass; salary threshold revision effective June 2026 (fetched June 2026)
IMI - Main services overview listing all visa, pass, and program categories (fetched June 2026)

IMI FAQ: Malaysia-China Mutual Visa Exemption Agreement - confirms permanent bilateral treaty in force 17 July 2025, 30 days/entry, max 90 days/180-day period (re-verified 2026-06-27; used as primary China source_url replacing kln.gov.my/chn_beijing which has SSL errors)
US Department of State - Malaysia travel information page. Confirms US citizens are visa-free for 90 days for tourism or business. Fetched 2026-06-29. Source for USA visa_free entry.

Embassy of Malaysia, Abu Dhabi - Visa requirements for foreigners (UAE nationals). Confirms UAE citizens do not require a visa for social visits not exceeding 90 days. (SSL error on direct WebFetch; content confirmed via Google search snippet from official kln.gov.my page on 2026-06-29.)

Consulate General of Malaysia, Dubai - Visa requirements for foreigners (UAE nationals). Corroborating source for UAE visa-free 90-day entry. (SSL error on direct WebFetch; confirmed via search snippet 2026-06-29.)

UK Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office - Malaysia travel advice: entry requirements. Confirms British citizens can stay 90 days on arrival without a visa. Fetched successfully 2026-06-29. Used as corroborating source for GBR entry and as alternate source_url for GBR visa_free entry.

outbound_official.asof: 2006-02-26 (v1.1, publicly extractable; v1.2 exists on same URL but requires portal login as of June 2026)
outbound_official.notes: Published by Malaysia Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Wisma Putra / kln.gov.my). Title: 'COUNTRIES THAT REQUIRE VISA / DO NOT REQUIRE VISA' - outbound visa requirements for Malaysian ordinary passport holders. The v1.1 PDF (creation date 2006-02-26) was publicly downloadable; v1.2 is the current portal version but redirects to login. The list below reflects v1.1 data; significant changes have occurred since (e.g. Australia, Japan, China, South Korea, USA now have different regimes for Malaysians). Use destination-side data for current accuracy. No eTA or visa-on-arrival categories were enumerated in the document; it classifies each country as either 'Visa Required' or 'Visa Not Required' for Malaysian ordinary passport holders.
